questions.question: The definition of pain, according to the World Health Organization is:
options: An alarm reaction of the organism (reflecting abnormality)
Unpleasant sensory or emotional experience associated with actual or potential tissue damage.
Defensive sensation, alarming for an imminent danger.
Physiological reaction of the CNS to unpleasant external or internal influences

questions.question: What is nociception?
options: Receiving, transmitting and perception of information for tissue damage.
A teaching for the mechanisms of cellular and tissue damage.

questions.question: From a physiological perspective pain is:
options: Multilevel complex reaction of the CNS
Sensation, characterizing precisely the qualities of the noxious stimulus

questions.question: The CNS interneurons with analgetic effect release:
Pain comprehension includes:
options: Localization and characterizing of the pain sensation.
Assessment of the positive and negative effects of pain.
The necessity of targeted and adequate behaviour.

questions.question: The "pain gate" in the posterior horns of the spinal cord "opens" in response to:
options: Afferent signals from A(α) and A(β) fibers.
Stable nociceptive afferent stimulation from A(δ) or C fibers.

questions.question: Unlike physiological, pathological pain is characterized by: (3)
options: causes the development of structural and functional changes and damage to internal organs
becomes pathogenic factor in the development of new pathological processes
as a rule, the temporary nature of the pain sensation

questions.question: Indicate biologically active substances and receptors that are important components of the nociceptive system: (4)
There are 4 types of brain edema: (4)
Pathological pain of peripheral origin occurs in: (4)
The (presence of) pain is: (1)
options: A mandatory symptom of every disease.
A signal for actual or potential tissue damage

questions.question: Disorders of the basal ganglia are characterized by(3)
Alterations in coordination of muscle movements and abnormal muscle movements result from disorders of:
Disorders of peripheral nerves include: (2)
options: reduction in striatal concentrations of dopamine

questions.question: Ischemic stroke, which is the most common type, is caused by: (2)
This statement is correct:
options: Communicating hydrocephalus results from impaired reabsorption of CSF from the arachnoid villi into the venous system
Communicating hydrocephalus occurs when obstruction in the ventricular system prevents the CSF from reaching the arachnoid villi.

questions.question: Cognitive disorders can be caused by: (1)
options: disorder that permanently damages large cortical or subcortical areas of the hemispheres
by the rupture of a blood vessel and bleeding into the brain
subsequent brain swelling, an intracranial hematoma

questions.question: Muscular dystrophy is a number of disorders that produce: (1)
options: damages large cortical or subcortical areas of the hemispheres
symmetric sensory, motor, and mixed sensorimotor deficits
reduction in striatal concentrations of dopamine

questions.question: Spinal cord injury is: (1)
options: impairment of regulatory mechanisms that maintain cerebral blood flow and oxygen supply
neurologic condition most commonly caused by motor vehicle accidents, falls, and sports injuries

questions.question: Parkinsonism is a syndrome of chronic progressive disorder of motor function and is clinically characterized by: (3)
